title: AOS/CP (Family) - Murthy Law Firm | U.S Immigration Law
content:
Adjustment of status or consular processing (AOS/CP) is the final stage of green card processing in both family- and employment-based immigration
- Adjustment of status (AOS) is available if the beneficiary is already in the United States.
- Consular processing (CP) is applicable if the qualifying relative is abroad and it involves an interview scheduled at a U.S. consulate.
- The Murthy Law Firm provides advice on legal matters and assists with all family-based immigration filings with local USCIS offices, service centers, and U.S. consulates.
- Murthy Law Firm attorneys assist with the preparation for and representation at an interview at the USCIS or consulate.

## For More on AOS/CP, See Permanent Resident FB Page
For AOS/CP news, information, complexities, and options, as well as Murthy Law Firm success stories in such cases, see the Family-Based, Permanent Resident page. You may also search the site for a particular topic, and narrow your search by category.
